name: Upper Navarrese Basque

ancestors.notes: Upper Navarrese uses the standard Basque grapheme base but exhibits systematic phonological reduction driven by Spanish contact, particularly in sibilant and lateral distinctions.

notes: Upper Navarrese (Goi-Nafar). Southern Basque dialect covering the Pamplona basin and surrounding areas of Navarre. Heavy Spanish contact has driven significant phonological changes: weakening and partial merger of the apical/laminal sibilant distinction, incipient yeísmo (ʎ → ʝ merger), and Spanish-like lenition patterns for voiced obstruents. Aspiration (/h/) is absent throughout. Lexical and morphological borrowing from Spanish is extensive. Considered highly endangered; the variety spoken around Pamplona underwent substantial language shift in the 20th century.

sources.author: Hualde, J.I. & Ortiz de Urbina, J. (eds.)
sources.title: A Grammar of Basque

sources.author: Ladefoged, P. & Maddieson, I.
sources.title: The Sounds of the World's Languages
